arguments.task: Open the official Amazon Selling Partner API developer documentation and extract only from first-party pages: 1) one-sentence description of the API/product, 2) all documented authentication methods for developers or applications, 3) any explicit access gating or self-serve onboarding requirements including seller/developer registration and app approval, 4) whether the API surface is REST and/or GraphQL, 5) rate limit notes, 6) whether Amazon provides an official MCP server or MCP documentation for this API. Prefer canonical docs pages, auth pages, API reference pages, and pricing/access pages. Return the supporting official URLs you actually read and concise evidence text for each.

input.thought: Launch browser research on official Amazon SP-API docs and related first-party pages.

arguments.task: Open only official Amazon Selling Partner API first-party pages and return exact evidence with canonical URLs. Visit these pages if available: What is the Selling Partner API, Authorizing Selling Partner API applications, Selling Partner API onboarding overview, Tokens API, and Usage Plans and Rate Limits in the Selling Partner API. For each page, extract short verbatim or near-verbatim evidence and the URL for: product description, documented auth methods (for example Login with Amazon OAuth, restricted data token, AWS SigV4 if documented), onboarding/access requirements, REST vs GraphQL API surface, rate limit model, and whether any official MCP server/docs exist. Avoid broken URLs and return only pages whose content loaded.

input.thought: Rerun with narrower page targets because one navigation hit a 404 and we need canonical live pages only.

raw_result.description: Amazon Selling Partner API lets developers build applications that help Amazon selling partners manage and automate their business operations.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official SP-API docs describe authorization via Login with Amazon (LWA) for application authorization and request signing via AWS Signature Version 4; the latter is a non-standard signing scheme mapped to 'other'. A Restricted Data Token is also documented for certain sensitive operations but is not treated here as a primary standalone auth method.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official onboarding documentation indicates developers must register as a Selling Partner API developer and sellers or vendors must authorize the application during onboarding; access is not presented as an anonymous open API and commonly requires registered developer/app setup before use.

api_surface.breadth: Broad ecommerce marketplace API covering orders, listings, catalog, inventory, reports, feeds, finances, notifications, tokens, uploads, and related seller operations.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official Amazon Selling Partner API MCP server or MCP documentation was evidenced in the official pages reviewed.

raw_result.blocker: Building is feasible from public docs, but first use requires Amazon developer registration, app setup, seller authorization, and AWS SigV4/LWA implementation rather than simple immediate self-serve API-key access.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Amazon documents usage plans and rate limits for SP-API operations, typically with operation-specific request quotas and burst values returned in headers; exact limits vary by API and operation.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs are substantial and first-party. Browser evidence showed a 404 on one attempted rate-limit path, so rate-limit detail is supported only at a high level from the doc set already reached rather than a confirmed canonical rate-limit article fetch.
raw_result.human_review_reason: A structured field remains partly uncertain from the pages already gathered: access_tier/buildability depend on how strictly Amazon's registration-and-authorization onboarding should be mapped between admin_approval_required and ready_with_workaround without an explicitly fetched pricing/access policy page, and rate-limit page confirmation hit a 404 during browsing.
